subtitle: When the Eastern Sea Bore the Tiger Banner

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of a royal flagship cutting across a moonlit ocean
panels.action: Rajendra Chola I stands firm at the prow of a massive carved warship, cloak and hair whipped by sea wind, as countless vessels stretch behind him over the dark, heaving Bay of Bengal under a star-filled sky.

dialogue.text: Rajendra Chola, lord of the Kaveri, sends a tiger-bannered armada across the rising eastern sea, to strike distant Srivijaya in the year of our Lord around 1025.

i18n.hi: कावेरी के स्वामी राजेन्द्र चोल ने बाघ-ध्वज वाला बेड़ा उठती पूर्वी लहरों पर भेजा, सन १०२५ के आसपास, दूर बैठे श्रीविजय पर वार करने।

panels.shot: wide interior of a temple hall, slightly low-angle, emphasizing grandeur
panels.action: Inside the Brihadisvara-like stone hall lit by oil lamps, Rajendra Chola I stands before a granite lingam, hands joined, as priests chant and nobles wait in respectful silence behind him.

dialogue.text: In Thanjavur’s stone womb of power, Rajendra, son of Rajaraja, bows before Shiva. Behind him, the Chola court waits for his next move after victories on land.

i18n.hi: तंजावुर की पत्थर की शक्ति-कोख में राजेन्द्र, राजराज का पुत्र, शिव के आगे झुकता है। पीछे चोल दरबार, ज़मीन की जीतों के बाद उसकी अगली चाल को टोह रहा है।

panels.shot: medium close-up side profile of Rajendra, contemplative, with map in foreground
panels.action: Rajendra Chola I leans over a palm-leaf map showing the Bay of Bengal and coasts marked with tiny lotus and ship symbols, his finger resting near the scribbled word 'Kadaram'.

dialogue.text: His armies have carried the tiger standard to the Ganga. Yet across the sea, Srivijaya holds the straits and tolls Chola trade, weighing his empire’s throat with unseen hands.

i18n.hi: उसकी सेनाएँ गंगा तक बाघ ध्वज पहुँचा चुकी हैं। पर समंदर के पार श्रीविजय जलडमरूमध्यों पर काबू रखकर, चोल व्यापार पर टैक्स लादकर, साम्राज्य के गले पर अदृश्य हाथ रखे है।

panels.shot: wide council-hall shot, elevated vantage, showing ministers and commanders around
panels.action: In a pillared audience hall, Rajendra Chola I sits on a low lion-throne, listening as grey-bearded ministers and mailed naval captains stand in a semicircle, one pointing toward a rolled-out maritime chart.

dialogue.text: Merchants murmur of seized cargoes and slighted envoys. Courtiers speak of prestige, rivals and routes. The sea, once a highway of pilgrims and pepper, has become a field of contest.

i18n.hi: व्यापारी जब्त माल और अपमानित दूतों की सुगबुगाहट करते हैं। दरबारी मान, दुश्मन और राहों की बात करते हैं। जो समंदर कभी तीर्थ और काली मिर्च की सड़क था, अब जंग का मैदान हो उठा है।

panels.shot: tight three-quarter view focusing on Rajendra rising from throne, commanding presence
panels.action: Rajendra Chola I rises from his throne, eyes steady, one hand resting on the hilt of his sword as courtiers fall silent around him in the lamplit hall.

dialogue.speaker: rajendra_chola_i
dialogue.text: If the straits choke our traders, we will open them with our oars.

i18n.hi: अगर जलडमरूमध्य हमारे व्यापारियों को घोंटेगा, तो हम अपनी पतवारों से उसे खोल देंगे।

panels.shot: sweeping bird's-eye over a busy Kaveri river wharf, teeming with ships and workers
panels.action: At a bustling riverine port on the Kaveri delta, rows of sturdy wooden ships are being caulked and loaded as elephants haul timber, shipwrights hammer planks, and soldiers drill in the background while Rajendra observes from a raised platform.

dialogue.text: From Nagapattinam to Kaveripattinam, shipyards awaken. Timber forests march into hulls, copper into nails, sailors from coasts and islands into ordered crews under the Chola seal.

i18n.hi: नागपट्टिनम से कावेरीपट्टिनम तक शिपयार्ड जाग उठते हैं। जंगल की लकड़ी धँसकर जहाज़ों में बदलती है, ताँबा कीलों में, तट और द्वीपों के नाविक चोल मुहर के नीचे पंक्तिबद्ध दलों में।

panels.shot: medium shot on Rajendra inspecting a flagship at dockside, slight low-angle
panels.action: Rajendra Chola I stands on the gangplank of a massive multi-oared flagship, examining its raised fighting deck and high stern while a naval commander salutes and rows of oarsmen sit ready at their benches.

dialogue.text: This is no coastal raid. It is an armada: long fighting ships with ranks of oars, high-sided transports for horses and men, supply craft heavy with grain, water and iron.

i18n.hi: ये बस तटीय लूट नहीं। ये पूरा बेड़ा है: लंबी युद्ध नौकाएँ घने चप्पुओं की कतारों के साथ, ऊँची ढाल वाली घुड़सवार और सैनिक ढोने वाली जहाज़, और अनाज, पानी, लोहे से लदी रसद नौकाएँ।

panels.shot: dramatic wide shot from behind Rajendra, looking out to sea at assembled fleet at dawn
panels.action: At dawn on the shoreline, Rajendra Chola I stands silhouetted against the pale horizon as dozens upon dozens of ships line the surf, their masts rising like a forest, crews waiting for the signal.

dialogue.text: The Bay of Bengal lies calm, but the horizon is crowded with masts. No Indian emperor before has gathered such force upon the water.

i18n.hi: बंगाल की खाड़ी शांत पड़ी है, पर क्षितिज मस्तूलों से भरा है। किसी भारतीय सम्राट ने पहले कभी इतना समुद्री बल एक जगह नहीं जोड़ा।

panels.shot: close-up on Rajendra’s hand raising a conch as men watch tensely
panels.action: On the beach, Rajendra Chola I lifts a polished conch to his lips, his arm steady, as disciplined ranks of marines and sailors watch him, their faces tense with expectation.

dialogue.speaker: rajendra_chola_i
dialogue.text: For trade, for honour, for the name of Chola—row east.

i18n.hi: व्यापार के लिए, सम्मान के लिए, चोल नाम के लिए—चप्पू पूर्व की ओर चलाओ।

panels.shot: high wide shot of the fleet leaving the Coromandel coast, coastline receding
panels.action: The vast Chola fleet moves in ordered lines over the open sea, white spray flying from the prows as the Tamil coastline dwindles behind, marked only by temples and palm groves fading into haze.

dialogue.text: Oars bite water in unison. Sails billow with monsoon winds. The fleet bears warriors, interpreters, pilots who know foreign currents, and the weight of a king’s decision.

i18n.hi: पतवार एक ताल में पानी काटते हैं। पाल मानसून की हवा से फूल उठते हैं। बेड़ा योद्धा, दुभाषिए, पराए पानी जानने वाले पायलट, और एक राजा के फैसले का पूरा भार ढो रहा है।

panels.shot: medium interior shot on Rajendra in the low-roofed captain’s cabin during storm
panels.action: Inside the flagship’s cramped cabin lit by swinging lamps, Rajendra Chola I braces himself against a timber beam as the ship tilts, water spray flying through a half-open hatch and crew rushing on the deck above.

dialogue.text: The eastern sea tests them with squalls, unseen sandbars and starless nights. Yet pilots read swells and winds, tracing routes long walked by merchants and monks.

i18n.hi: पूर्वी सागर उन्हें आँधियों, छुपी रेती और बेतारों रातों से परखता है। पर पायलट लहरों और हवा को पढ़ते हैं, वही राहें पकड़ते जो सदियों से व्यापारी और भिक्षु नापते आए हैं।

panels.shot: wide shot of fleet approaching a foreign island coast fringed with forests
panels.action: Under a cloudy sky, the Chola fleet nears a lush island coastline with dense tropical forest and a river mouth, small local boats scurrying out of the way as the warships advance.

dialogue.text: First come islands and lesser ports: waystations that mark the long arc toward Sumatra and the Malay coast, where Srivijaya’s power rests on harbours and river mouths.

i18n.hi: पहले द्वीप और छोटे बंदरगाह मिलते हैं—लंबी चाप के मील-पत्थर, जो सुमात्रा और मलय तट की ओर दिखाते हैं, जहाँ श्रीविजय की ताकत बंदरगाहों और नदी-मुखों पर टिकी है।

panels.shot: medium shot from behind Rajendra as he watches Srivijayan harbor in the distance
panels.action: Rajendra Chola I stands on the raised aft deck, hands clasped behind his back, watching a distant Srivijayan harbor with its own ships and wooden palisades come into view through the morning mist.

dialogue.text: Inscriptions at his capital will later remember these names: Sri Vijaya, Kadaram, and other ports, bound not by temples alone but by tolls, warehouses and armed quays.

i18n.hi: राजधानी की शिलालेख बाद में इन नामों को याद करेंगे: श्री विजय, कदारम् और और भी बंदरगाह, जो सिर्फ मंदिरों से नहीं, टोल, गोदाम और हथियारबंद घाटों से बँधे थे।

panels.shot: dynamic wide shot of Chola ships closing in on a fortified harbor, angled from above
panels.action: From a high vantage, Chola warships surge toward a riverine harbour lined with wooden defenses and watchtowers, local defenders scrambling while merchants’ vessels lie anchored helplessly inside.

dialogue.text: Battle comes swift by water. Chola ships drive into harbours, break chains and barriers, seize anchorages. The blow is aimed at power and revenue, not at empty boasting.

i18n.hi: समंदर पर जंग बिजली-सी टूटती है। चोल जहाज़ बंदरगाहों में घुसकर जंजीरें, रुकावटें तोड़ते हैं, लंगरगाहों पर कब्जा करते हैं। वार खाली डींग पर नहीं, शक्ति और राजस्व पर है।

panels.shot: medium shot on Rajendra on deck amidst ordered chaos, gesturing commands
panels.action: On the flagship’s deck amid rushing sailors and marines, Rajendra Chola I stands calm, signaling with his arm toward the harbour while smoke from burning stockpiles rises in the distance.

dialogue.text: Srivijaya, master of straits and entrepôts, is struck in its heartlands. Coedes would later write of an Indian king whose fleet cut through the island empire’s delicate web of control.

i18n.hi: जलडमरूमध्य और बंदरगाहों का उस्ताद श्रीविजय अपने दिल में चोट खाता है। बाद में कोइदेस लिखेगा उस भारतीय राजा के बारे में जिसकी नौसेना ने इस द्वीपी साम्राज्य के नाज़ुक जाल को चीर दिया।

panels.shot: wide shot of Chola troops disembarking at a Malay port, jungle in background
panels.action: Armoured Chola soldiers descend gangplanks onto the muddy shore of a Malay port, lines of palm trees and dense jungle beyond, as officers point inland toward a settlement of wooden halls.

dialogue.text: At Kadaram, gateway of the Malay Peninsula, his men come ashore. They do not stay to rule its jungles, but to show that the sea-roads cannot be closed to the Kaveri.

i18n.hi: मलय प्रायद्वीप के फाटक कदारम् में उसके लोग तट पर उतरते हैं। वे जंगल राज करने नहीं रुकते, बल्कि दिखाने कि कावेरी के लिए समुद्री राहें बंद नहीं की जा सकतीं।

panels.shot: medium shot aboard ship showing captured foreign envoys and merchants under guard
panels.action: On a Chola vessel’s deck, a few richly dressed foreign officials and merchants sit guarded yet unharmed, their faces a mix of fear and calculation, as Rajendra Chola I speaks with an interpreter between them.

dialogue.text: Ports fall, allies are sought, rivals warned. The expedition is a message: that the bay is not Srivijaya’s lake alone, and that distant kings must account for southern interests.

i18n.hi: बंदरगाह गिरते हैं, दोस्त ढूँढ़े जाते हैं, दुश्मनों को चेतावनी मिलती है। यह अभियान एक संदेश है: कि खाड़ी सिर्फ श्रीविजय की झील नहीं, और दूर के राजा भी दक्षिण की दिलचस्पियों का हिसाब देंगे।

panels.shot: solemn wide shot of the fleet turning homeward at sea, with foreign coastline fading
panels.action: The Chola fleet turns away from the foreign coastline, sails angled westward, as the lush mountains of Sumatra or the Malay Peninsula recede slowly into the mist behind them.

dialogue.text: Then, as swiftly as it came, the armada withdraws. Rajendra’s goal is struck: prestige won, trade routes shaken, Srivijaya’s aura of untouchable sea-power cracked.

i18n.hi: और जितनी जल्दी वह आया था, उतनी ही जल्दी बेड़ा लौट भी जाता है। राजेन्द्र का निशाना साधा गया—मान मिला, व्यापार मार्ग हिल गए, श्रीविजय की अजेय समुद्री सत्ता की चमक चटक गई।

panels.shot: ceremonial wide shot inside a Chola temple hall, focus on inscription being carved
panels.action: Inside a temple hall back in the Chola realm, a royal scribe chisels careful lines onto a stone slab listing far-off place-names, while Rajendra Chola I watches, hands resting lightly on the railing.

dialogue.text: In Rajendra’s Tanjore inscription, the captured or humbled ports are carved in stone. Stone outlives storm and voyage; it tells posterity that an Indian fleet once carried war beyond India’s shores.

i18n.hi: राजेन्द्र के तंजावुर शिलालेख में वो बंदरगाह खुदे हैं जो जीते या झुकाए गए। पत्थर आँधी और यात्रा से लंबा जीता है; वह आने वाली पीढ़ी को बताता है कि कभी भारतीय बेड़े ने भारत तट से परे युद्ध ढोया था।

panels.shot: medium close-up of an older Rajendra sitting reflective in his palace garden at dusk
panels.action: In a quiet palace garden at dusk, an older Rajendra Chola I sits on a stone bench, listening to the distant murmur of the city, his gaze turned toward the east as palm fronds sway gently.

dialogue.text: He has bathed his name in the Ganga, sent his flag to Malay seas. Yet even for him, the eastern ocean remains a road of influence, not a field of lasting rule.

i18n.hi: उसने अपना नाम गंगा में नहलाया, अपना ध्वज मलय सागरों तक भेजा। फिर भी उसके लिए भी पूर्वी महासागर सिर्फ असर की राह है, स्थायी राज का मैदान नहीं।

panels.shot: final wide shot of the Bay of Bengal at sunrise with faint outline of temples and ships
panels.action: The sun rises over the calm Bay of Bengal, distant silhouettes of Chola temples on one shore and small trading ships heading east, the reflection of light making a golden path across the water.

dialogue.text: Rajendra’s armada shows how vision and ships can carry a kingdom’s reach across horizons. Yet it also whispers that power flung far must be guided by wisdom, lest waves erase its marks.

i18n.hi: राजेन्द्र का बेड़ा दिखाता है कि दूरदर्शिता और जहाज़ मिलकर एक राज्य की बाँह क्षितिज के पार फैला सकते हैं। पर यह ये भी फुसफुसाता है कि दूर फेंकी ताकत को बुद्धि सँभाले, नहीं तो लहरें उसके निशान धो डालेंगी।

title_i18n.hi: राजेन्द्र का बेड़ा श्रीविजय की ओर

subtitle_i18n.en: When the Eastern Sea Bore the Tiger Banner
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब पूर्वी सागर ने बाघ ध्वज ढोया
